metadata.title: Conversación y Vocabulario: ¿Dónde viven ustedes?
metadata.description: A classroom conversation about where the students live and their family, plus vocabulary from page 207.

sentences.original: Alumno: Vivimos en la calle Bolívar.
sentences.translation: Student: We live on Bolivar Street.
Teacher: What work does your father do?

sentences.original: Alumna: Nuestro papá trabaja en una oficina.
sentences.translation: Student: Our father works in an office.

sentences.original: Profesora: ¿Hay otras personas en la familia?
sentences.translation: Teacher: Are there other people in the family?

sentences.original: Alumno: Sí, nuestros abuelos viven con nosotros.
sentences.translation: Student: Yes, our grandparents live with us.
Teacher: Do you live in an apartment?

sentences.original: Alumno: ¡Oh, no! Para nuestra familia necesitamos una casa grande.
sentences.translation: Student: Oh, no! For our family we need a big house.

sentences.original: La calle es muy bonita y tranquila.
sentences.translation: The street is very pretty and quiet.

sentences.original: Esta es la calle principal de la ciudad.
sentences.translation: This is the main street of the city.
Mr. Lopez, is this your car?

sentences.original: Usted es profesor y su clase es excelente.
sentences.translation: You are a teacher and your class is excellent.

sentences.original: El libro de español es para el alumno nuevo.
sentences.translation: The Spanish book is for the new student.

sentences.original: Este regalo es para mi abuela.
sentences.translation: This gift is for my grandmother.

sentences.original: Nuestro apartamento es pequeño pero cómodo.
sentences.translation: Our apartment is small but comfortable.

sentences.original: Ella es nuestra tía de México.
sentences.translation: She is our aunt from Mexico.
